title: Use returndatasize() to verify precompile or ecrecover call success
description: After a staticcall to ecrecover (precompile 0x1) or other precompiles, returndatasize() is 0x20 (32 bytes) on success and 0x00 on failure. This provides a cheaper success check than inspecting the return value of staticcall itself, and can be combined with other conditions using iszero() for compact error handling.
pattern_before:
let success := staticcall(gas(), 1, 0x00, 0x80, 0x20, 0x20)
if iszero(success) { revert(0, 0) }
when_to_apply: After staticcall to precompiles (ecrecover, SHA256, identity) where returndatasize indicates success
when_not_to_apply: Regular contract calls where returndatasize varies based on function return type
